title: Proofpoint Email DLP Policy Match Rate by Rule and Severity
dataSources: `sourcetype=proofpoint:pps:dlp` Proofpoint Protection Server DLP violation or policy match events
spl:
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint:pps:dlp" earliest=-14d
| eval rule=coalesce(rule_name, Rule_Name, policy, Policy, "unknown")
| eval sev=coalesce(severity, Severity, "medium")
| eval action=coalesce(action, Action, "")
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count by _time rule sev
| eventstats sum(count) as daily_total by _time
| eval pct=round(100*count/daily_total,3)
| sort _time -count
description: Email DLP rules that fire too often create alert fatigue; rules that never fire may be misconfigured. Daily match rates by rule and severity balance privacy operations workload against coverage.
value: Improves DLP program maturity with data-driven tuning and gives compliance stakeholders transparent metrics on outbound data protection.
implementation: (1) Forward PPS DLP events with rule and severity fields. (2) Map rules to data classes (PCI, PHI, IP). (3) Set thresholds for sudden drops (possible logging break) or spikes (possible campaign). (4) Review with legal quarterly.
visualization: Stacked area (matches over time by severity), heatmap (rule × day), table with percentage of daily volume.

references.title: Splunkbase — Splunk Add-on for Proofpoint On Demand

grandmaExplanation: We watch how Proofpoint treats and rewrites mail, so threats and DLP events line up with what the security team already sees in their console.
knownFalsePositives: Rule changes, DLP test traffic, and compliance sampling that raises match counts for a time.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable; primary SPL uses raw or vendor-normalized event fields.
